gaps: No official API documentation — all endpoints are undocumented
Rate limits are per-IP, aggressive for bulk use
Feb 2026 reputation risk: Wikipedia blacklisted archive.today over DDoS and tampering
patterns: No authentication required for any endpoint

novel_features.name: Read article as markdown
novel_features.description: Fetch the archived page and extract clean markdown text — ready to pipe into Claude, LLMs, or notes.
novel_features.rationale: No existing CLI extracts article text from archive snapshots. Requires fetching the memento, running readability extraction, and markdownifying the result.

novel_features.name: Lookup-before-submit
novel_features.description: Find existing snapshot with timegate first, submit only if nothing recent exists. Saves 60+ seconds per hit and avoids rate limits.
novel_features.rationale: Every competing tool submits on every call. Checking timegate first (a 500ms lookup vs. a 60-second submit) is only possible because we're optimizing the agent workflow, not mirroring a Python wrapper.

novel_features.name: Mirror auto-fallback
novel_features.description: Automatically retry across archive.ph, archive.md, archive.is, archive.fo, and other mirrors when one is blocked or rate-limited.
novel_features.rationale: Only possible because we maintain our own transport layer with per-mirror state. No competing tool does this.

novel_features.name: Dual-backend fallback
novel_features.description: When archive.is fails or is rate-limited, automatically try Wayback Machine. Hedges the February 2026 archive.today reputation risk.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a backend abstraction that covers both archive.today's Memento API and Wayback's JSON availability API. No other CLI bridges these two services.

novel_features.name: Local FTS5 search
novel_features.description: Full-text search across your past archive reads — find that article you saved last month without remembering the URL.
novel_features.rationale: Requires extracting article text at read time and indexing it locally. No competing tool persists article text; they only persist URLs.

novel_features.name: Bulk archive with rate-limit awareness
novel_features.description: Archive a list of URLs from a file or stdin, with built-in 10-second gaps and exponential backoff on 429s. Run it and walk away.
novel_features.rationale: Requires knowing archive.is's rate-limit behavior and modeling it in the client. Competing tools either serialize with no backoff or burn through quota in seconds.
